items.title: Dingo control stopped to protect cultural significance

children.content: Farmers voice concern for their flocks after wild dog control is halted on jointly managed conservation land in parts of the Wheatbelt.

items.title: Parents say latest asbestos find in WA school grounds is one of many

children.content: Parents at a WA primary school say the education department has failed to properly address the ongoing issue of asbestos being found in school playgrounds.

image.altText: catherine mayfield

items.title: 'Life for a life': Family of Thai teen wants alleged killer to face death penalty

children.content: The family of a Thai teenager allegedly killed by an Australian man has placed her cremated ashes to rest at a local Buddhist temple as they seek justice for her murder.

image.altText: A thai woman in  a beige shirt holds a white photo frame.

items.title: Matthew Pavlich reflects on playing little-known first Pride game a decade ago

children.content: This Friday will mark 10 years of Sydney Swans Pride games for premiership points.

image.altText: Matthew Pavlich marched in this year's Mardi Gras for the Sydney Swans

items.title: Tough new 'fit and proper person' tests for WA tow truck operators

children.content: The new laws come into effect from today, including "fit and proper person" tests for business operators.

image.altText: A tow truck sits on a flatbed truck.

items.title: Alcoa to buy South32's WA bauxite mine in $8b deal

children.content: American mining giant Alcoa is set to buy $8 billion worth of assets from Australia's South32. The local shire and workers' union say they were blindsided by the deal.

gallery.altText: Massive industrial crusher surrounded by strip mined land and red dirt

thumbnail.altText: Massive industrial crusher surrounded by strip mined land and red dirt

items.title: ACCC blocks Coles supermarket in Kalgoorlie to protect regional city's independents

children.content: In an unprecedented move, Australia's consumer watchdog exercises new powers to thwart the opening of a Coles supermarket in the WA outback city, fearing it could knock out smaller competitors in the wider region.

gallery.altText: Shopping trolleys in a parking lot for a supermarket.
Street signs with bushland in the background.
The owner of a supermarket standing in fruit and vegetable isle with arms crossed.

items.title: Government backs down on aged care tool as MPs stun at Midwinter Ball

children.content: The federal government has backed down on its aged care assessment tool to determine how much funding older Australians get. Follow live.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese and Jodie Haydon walk to Midwinter Ball.

items.title: Influencers to be targeted in crackdown on gambling advertising

children.content: The Albanese government will target social media influencers and individuals who promote gambling on social media as part of its proposed laws restricting advertising.

image.altText: A woman with the parliament house flag in the background.

items.title: When working more hours might mean you lose your home

children.content: Fremantle resident Trish Owen could lose the tenancy on her government-provided home if she works more hours — and a new study suggests many people are in the same situation.

items.title: Thousands of survivors of child sexual abuse face looming redress backlog

children.content: At its current processing rate, the scheme will not be able to clear its backlog before it closes.

image.altText: Chris Coombes

items.title: NSW sergeant 'snuck a look' for ex-cop mate accused of domestic violence

children.content: A NSW Police officer has admitted to unlawfully accessing the force's internal database to locate details about a domestic violence complaint against his friend.

image.altText: Photo illustration with police officer silhouette and text messages from story

items.title: Djokovic apologises for pranking ball kid during 'vintage' Wimbledon win

children.content: A relaxed Novak Djokovic takes time out between sets of his second-round Wimbledon win over Stefanos Tsitsipas to play a prank on a ball kid.

image.altText: Novak Djokovic pranks a ball kid at Wimbledon.

items.title: Jackass began with a gun shot; now it's a $500m franchise

children.content: For more than 25 years, prank franchise Jackass has been delighting teens and terrifying parents. Now the gang is heading to the cinemas for what could be their last ride.

image.altText: A bull gores a man dressed as a matador

items.title: BHP shows off two electric trucks — with 62 diesel ones on order

children.content: BHP and Rio Tinto have put their two battery-electric trucks on show in Western Australia's Pilbara, but is an aversion to risk stifling meaningful change?

items.title: Matthew Flinders’s steamy letters reveal a hidden side to the man who named Australia

children.content: Flinders circumnavigated and named Australia. Impressive stuff, but he also wrote many impassioned letters and lived a life full of crushes.

image.altText: A letter from 1800, cursive ink writing and faded paper

items.title: Nobody knows where John Frum first came from, but he is god in this village

children.content: On Vanuatu's Tanna Island, I immerse myself in a culture that worships a shape-shifting spirit known as John Frum.

image.altText: Kate dances with a woman in a field in Vanuatu.

items.title: Iconic Gloucester Tree reopens after three-year closure

children.content: Tourism operators are celebrating the reopening of the southern WA landmark, the Gloucester Tree, with visitors making the 37-metre climb to its peak for the first time since its closure three years ago.

gallery.altText: A low angle shot of a tree with spiral planks leading up to the very top of the tree and its leaves

thumbnail.altText: A low angle shot of a tree with spiral planks leading up to the very top of the tree and its leaves

items.title: Extremists using 'codes' to evade detection online, royal commission hears

children.content: Far-right individuals adopt memes as a way to push "more palatable" antisemitism, which is less likely to be picked up by social media moderators.

image.altText: Hannah Rose in a blazer, with brunette hair, speaking before the commission.

items.title: ASX opens lower as the price of oil drops

children.content: The ASX opens lower following a sea of red on Wall Street. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: Picture of stock market board with ASX sign at the top left corner.

items.title: XPeng electric vehicle owners still chasing $5k cashback months on

children.content: When Mustansir Milky took the leap and bought a new electric vehicle, he didn't expect to have to fight to get the $5,000 cashback payment he was promised.

image.altText: Mustansir Milky stands beside his electric car outside his house

items.title: The US and UK have banned kids heading the ball. Should Australia follow?

children.content: With all eyes on the World Cup, Australian football's management of the risks associated with the practice of heading the ball, such as dementia and CTE, is in the spotlight.

image.altText: A young soccer player about to kick a soccer ball.

items.title: Family appeals for help to find Perth man missing in Mexico for almost a month

children.content: Jesse Bruzzese's family say the 35-year-old solo traveller has not been in touch with them since arriving in Mexico at the beginning of June.

items.title: Pat Conroy to announce defence shake-up aimed at cost blowouts

children.content: Defence Industry Minister Pat Conroy is set to appeal to the Labor faithful to get behind AUKUS as he announces a defence shake-up targeting wasteful spending.

image.altText: A man with short hair and black rimmed glasses, talking.

items.title: Paediatrician accused of child sex offences now facing 53 charges

children.content: A Perth paediatrician accused of using hidden cameras to record himself abusing children is now facing more than 50 charges involving 10 alleged victims, with a court told further charges are likely to be laid.

image.altText: A head and shoulders shot of an Asian-Australian man, with black hair, wearing spectacles and a blue and white business shirt.

items.title: Basketball coach abused girl at primary school during training session, court told

children.content: Shannon Trew, 42, indecently dealt with a young girl he was training at a primary school, a Perth court hears.

image.altText: A close-up side-on shot of a middle-aged man with short hair.

items.title: French Open champ Mirra Andreeva left in tears after early Wimbledon exit

children.content: French Open champion Mirra Andreeva is reduced to tears after her Wimbledon run is ended in the second round by former winner Barbora Krejčíková.

image.altText: Mirra Andreeva wipes her face during a tennis match at Wimbledon.

items.title: Whale census records unexplained drop in sightings off WA's South West

children.content: Marine scientists have expressed concerns following a 60 per cent drop in migrating whales seen off South West WA's coastline compared to previous years.
